purpose: Same-machine RX 7900 XTX PARO regression spot-check: v0.1.1/pre-GGUF tag vs current tree after GGUF merge.
environment: Both runs used /home/lhl/miniforge3/envs/therock/bin/python and TheRock HIP 7.13.60940-478a7a43c6 LD/PATH setup; single-run diagnostic with graph replay decode and AOTriton threshold 512.
conclusion: No evidence of a GGUF-merge code regression in same-environment 512/128 or 4K/128 PARO: current prefill is -0.61% at both shapes, current decode is +2.75% at 512/128 and -0.18% at 4K/128. The larger gap versus older v0.1.1 W7900 artifacts and 2026-05-21 RX diagnostics is likely run environment, clocks, or measurement protocol, not code from the GGUF merge.

commands.v0_1_1_template: git worktree add --detach /tmp/hipengine-v0.1.1-paro-check v0.1.1; cd /tmp/hipengine-v0.1.1-paro-check; PYTHONPATH=$PWD $PY scripts/qwen35_paro_bench.py --model $PARO --prompt-length <512|4096> --token-id 9707 --decode-tokens 128 --warmup-decode-tokens 1 --compiler-version-file /tmp/hipengine-v011-hipcc-version.txt --attn-aotriton-min-tokens 512 --graph-replay-decode --json <tmp-json>

artifact_policy_note: Raw qwen35_paro_bench JSON files were not retained because they are verbose; this compact artifact preserves the measured fields and exact command templates needed for the regression decision.
